tagline: The Confluence connector in Lakeflow Connect is now Generally Available.
what_it_does: The Confluence connector in Lakeflow Connect is now Generally Available. The managed Confluence connector in Lakeflow Connect allows you to ingest data from Confluence into Databricks. Feature availability | Feature | Availability | | | | | UI based pipeline authoring | Supported | | API based pipeline authoring | Supported | | Declarative Automation Bundles | Supported | | Incremental ingestion | Supported | | Unity Catalog governance | Supported | | Orchestration using Databricks Workflows | Supported | | SCD type 2 | Supported | | API based column selection and deselection | Supported | | API based row filtering | Not supported | | Automated schema evolution: New and deleted columns | Supported | | Automated schema evolution: Data type changes | Not supported | | Automated schema evolution: Column renames | Supported Treated as a new column (new name) and deleted column (old name).
why_it_matters: Feature availability | Feature | Availability | | | | | UI based pipeline authoring | Supported | | API based pipeline authoring | Supported | | Declarative Automation Bundles | Supported | | Incremental ingestion | Supported | | Unity Catalog governance | Supported | | Orchestration using Databricks Workflows | Supported | | SCD type 2 | Supported | | API based column selection and deselection | Supported | | API based row filtering | Not supported | | Automated schema evolution: New and deleted columns | Supported | | Automated schema evolution: Data type changes | Not supported | | Automated schema evolution: Column renames | Supported Treated as a new column (new name) and deleted column (old name).
key_takeaway: The Confluence connector in Lakeflow Connect is now Generally Available.
